source: NvChad - NvChad UI: NvChad Interface: the top buffer bar, buffer status icons, splits and the bottom status line

questions.q: What is the top bar in NvChad called, and what does it do?
questions.why: The Tabufline manages open buffers at the top.

options: only when you have more than one file open
questions.why: It only shows with more than one buffer open.

questions.q: In a buffer's status icon, what do a red x and a green dot mean?
options: red x = file is saved (safe to close); green dot = unsaved changes
red x = an error; green dot = ok
red x = unsaved; green dot = saved
both mean the file is modified
questions.why: Red x means saved; green dot means it needs saving.

questions.q: How do you work on two files side by side?
options: Split the buffer with :vsplit and pick the second file in the tabufline
questions.why: :vsplit then selecting the other file gives side-by-side editing.

questions.q: Which are the valid editor states shown in the Statusline?
questions.why: Neovim's modes: NORMAL, INSERT, COMMAND, VISUAL.

questions.q: On the right, the statusline shows the folder you opened from, which for LSP is...
questions.why: That folder is the LSP workspace used for diagnostics.

questions.q: Git status indicators in the statusline are provided by...
